column87: This can be used with cover data and on >30 quadrats, less quadrats will be biased

column14: Quadrat sampling (presence, no presence, uniqueness). Since we do not have count of individual per species, this calculation will estimate the species richness from presence or absence data. For every quadrat, if there was percent cover recorded for a species, then 1 will be returned, if there was 0% cover of the species, then it will return 0. Schao is the estimator, and depeneds on the rare species observed. So it depends on the species that only have one observation and the species that have 2 observations.  Obtained from: https://www.zoology.ubc.ca/~krebs/downloads/krebs_chapter_13_2017.pdf
column49: Returned percent cover per specie on each quadrat. Then calculated the average percent cover for each species from all quadrats. From this calculated the relative abundace by dividing the average cover per species over the total cover
